steps.title: Identify the finding
steps.content: Participants recruited via snowball sampling — i.e., recruited by someone in their social network — had a much higher retention rate than those recruited by strangers. The blank should explain why this difference exists.

steps.title: Match the logical inference
steps.content:
Choice A explains the difference: being recruited by someone you know socially creates a sense of obligation that keeps you engaged. B is about who gets recruited (not retention), and C and D add a rural/nonrural comparison the study didn't make.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
